intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens; only a single, struggling stem remains in the black pot. P3 (Pothos) is present with the white rabbit anchor.

inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss (No evidence of Mexican Mint)
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No evidence of Silver Guest)

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot. Soil appears dark and undisturbed.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Combined: Single, thin, chlorotic stem remaining in the black pot. High probability of failure.
plant_audit.P3: Single leaf visible. Appears stable but isolated.

biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in the black pot (P2/P4/P3 area), indicating a calcium-supplementation intervention.
biome_observations.surface: Desk surface is clear of organic matter, save for the white paper/cabling.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t5: Initial state shows a single green leaf in the black pot and a bare yellow pot.
temporal_deltas.t5_to_current: The stem in the black pot has shown minimal postural change, suggesting a state of stasis or slow decline. No new growth observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe decline. The lack of foliage in P1 and the singular, fragile stem in the black pot suggest poor light conditions or root-zone failure. The 'Systemic Loss' of the majority of the registered species is confirmed.
anomalies: Sensor V2.0 tag in P1 (New Introduction)
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a failing indoor botanical setup. The registry is mostly aspirational, as the physical reality shows a near-total loss of the expected specimens. The only surviving plant matter is a single, isolated leaf in the P3/Pothos zone and a solitary, struggling stem in the black pot. The presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at soil amendment, but the lack of vegetative response indicates the plants are likely beyond recovery.
